channel.title: Angioedema on Angioedema Info
channel.description: Recent content in Angioedema on Angioedema Info

item.title: Diagnosis
item.description:
Diagnosis

Doctors diagnose angioedema by reviewing symptoms and checking for triggers or underlying causes.

🔬 Common tests and evaluations
  • Medical history and symptom review
  • Physical exam to identify swelling patterns
  • Blood tests for immune markers or hereditary factors
  • Imaging in rare cases when deeper tissue is involved
🧠 What doctors look for
  • Sudden swelling without rash
  • Recurrent episodes of skin or airway swelling
  • Possible triggers such as medications, food, or stress
  • Family history of hereditary angioedema
✨ When a diagnosis is urgent
  • Swelling affects the throat or breathing
  • Rapid onset of symptoms
  • Recurrent unexplained angioedema
  • Severe discomfort or pain

item.title: Symptoms
item.description:
Common Symptoms of Angioedema

Angioedema can present with a variety of symptoms depending on the affected area and the underlying cause. Here are the most common signs to watch for:

👤 Facial and Oral Symptoms
  • Swelling of the lips, tongue, or cheeks
  • Puffiness around the eyes or eyelids
  • Difficulty speaking or swallowing if the tongue is affected
  • Changes in facial appearance
🫁 Respiratory Symptoms
  • Swelling of the throat or airways
  • Difficulty breathing or shortness of breath
  • Wheezing or stridor (high-pitched breathing sound)
  • Feeling of tightness in the throat
👐 Skin and Extremity Symptoms
  • Swelling of hands, feet, or genitals
  • Hives or welts on the skin surface
  • Itching or burning sensation
  • Pain or discomfort in swollen areas
🚨 Emergency Warning Signs

Seek immediate medical attention if you experience:

item.title: Treatment
item.description:
Treatment

Treatment depends on the cause and how serious the swelling is.

🚑 Emergency care
  • Epinephrine for allergic reactions
  • Airway support if breathing is blocked
  • Hospital observation for severe swelling
  • Rapid treatment for throat or tongue involvement
💊 Common treatments
  • Antihistamines for allergy-related swelling
  • Steroids for inflammation
  • C1 inhibitor medicines for hereditary angioedema
  • Avoiding known triggers when possible
🧘 Long-term management
  • Keep a symptom diary
  • Carry emergency medication if prescribed
  • Know your triggers and avoid them
  • Stay in contact with your healthcare provider

Note

If you have questions about your care, talk with a doctor or specialist.

item.title: What is Angioedema?
item.description:
Overview

Angioedema is a swelling of the deeper layers of the skin, often affecting the face, lips, tongue, throat, hands, feet, or genitals. It may occur suddenly and can be potentially serious if it affects breathing.

🔍 Click to learn more about Causes

Causes

  • Allergic reactions (foods, medications, insect stings)
  • Non-allergic drug reactions (for example, ACE inhibitors)
  • Hereditary angioedema (genetic forms)
  • Idiopathic (no clear cause)
📋 Click to see Symptoms

Symptoms

  • Sudden swelling under the skin
  • Swelling of lips, eyelids, tongue, or throat
  • Pain or discomfort in the swollen area
  • Sometimes hives (urticaria) on the skin
🚨 Click for Emergency Care Information

When to seek emergency care

Call emergency services or go to the nearest hospital if you have:
